responsibilities: Design and develop embedded firmware in C/C++ for microcontroller-based products across automotive, industrial, or IoT domains.
Implement and optimize real-time operating system (RTOS) tasks, schedulers, and interrupt service routines for deterministic system behavior.
Develop and debug low-level device drivers for peripherals including SPI, I2C, UART, ADC, and GPIO interfaces.
Integrate embedded software with custom hardware boards, performing bring-up, debugging, and validation using JTAG and logic analyzers.
Collaborate with hardware engineers to define memory maps, interrupt architecture, and power management strategies for embedded systems.
Build and maintain bootloaders and firmware update mechanisms supporting secure over-the-air (OTA) updates.
Optimize embedded code for memory footprint, power consumption, and real-time performance constraints on resource-limited devices.
Conduct unit testing, hardware-in-the-loop testing, and static code analysis to ensure firmware reliability and quality.
Implement communication protocol stacks such as Modbus, CAN, BLE, and MQTT for device-to-device and cloud connectivity.
Document firmware architecture, design decisions, and test results to support certification and production handoff.
